data.description:
## Why

The audit (jscpd + manual reading, all 5 pairs re-confirmed by the validator)
found genuine logic duplication — not the intentional parallel-by-design of the
three interfaces, but real copy-paste where a bug must be fixed in two places.
Two of these are concurrency-/correctness-critical (the advisory-lock CAS twins
and the blocker-edge inversion). DRYing them removes a class of
fix-one-forget-the-other bugs.

## What

Extract shared helpers for five confirmed duplication clusters, ordered by
value-to-risk: F9 (LLM finalize bypass) → F7 (ticket-schema dict) → F5 (blocker
inversion) → F2/F3 (adopt the existing `_seam` helpers) → F4 (advisory-lock CAS
frame).

## Scope

- In scope: the specific call sites below. Each extraction stays within one
  layer/package (low coupling).
- Out of scope: collapsing the three interfaces' *parallel typed signatures*
  (intentional — do NOT `**kwargs` them); the reconciler inbound/outbound differs
  that genuinely *invert* (not duplicates).

## Context (citations)

- **F9 — LLM finalization bypass (lowest risk, helper already exists):**
  `src/rebar/llm/findings.py:209` (`finalize_findings`) is documented as *the*
  single `review_result` finalization strategy, but `src/rebar/llm/code_review.py:167-177`
  and `src/rebar/llm/spec_scan.py:102-113` re-hand-roll the identical
  `build_result → resolve_citations → validate_result` triad. Also `_SEVERITY_RANK`
  is byte-identical at `src/rebar/llm/aggregate.py:19` and
  `src/rebar/llm/spec_scan.py:20` (aggregate.py even exposes a `_severity_rank()`
  accessor).
- **F7 — ticket-schema dict literals:** `src/rebar/reducer/_state.py:8-31`
  (`make_initial_state`) and `:40-63` (`make_error_dict`) spell the ~20-field
  ticket schema as two near-identical literals. **Live drift already present:**
  `make_error_dict` omits `preconditions_summary` (in `make_initial_state` at
  ≈line 29) — exactly the bug this duplication invites.
- **F5 — blocker-edge inversion (correctness invariant, 2 copies):**
  `src/rebar/graph/_ready.py:76-91` and `src/rebar/graph/_unblock.py:91-104` both
  build the `blocked_by` map by walking `deps` and inverting
  `depends_on`/`blocks`. A relation-direction bug must be fixed in both.
- **F2/F3 — `_seam` re-inlining + drift:** `_commands/composer.py` and
  `_commands/leaf.py` correctly call `_seam.require_id` + `_seam.require_not_ghost`
  (`_commands/_seam.py:70-83`), but `_commands/transition.py` hand-inlines the
  resolve-id and the CREATE/SNAPSHOT ghost check (`transition_compute` ≈300-310,
  `claim_compute` ≈612-620). `_commands/compact.py:186` has **drifted** — it emits
  a bare stderr "not found" with no `error_envelope` JSON, unlike the
  `ticket_not_found` envelope transition/claim emit.
- **F4 — advisory-lock CAS twins (concurrency-critical):**
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py:500-590` and `:609-686`
  are the same snapshot → stale-worktree preflight → `worktree add --detach` → CAS
  `update-ref` → finally-cleanup frame, differing only in the inner mutate (write+
  add+commit vs rm+commit) plus the write path's staged-changes idempotency guard.

## Implementation plan

1. **F9:** make `code_review.py` and `spec_scan.py` call `findings.finalize_findings`
   instead of the inline triad (match its signature; pass the provenance kwargs).
   Replace `spec_scan._SEVERITY_RANK` with `aggregate._severity_rank()` (or import
   the constant). Run `tests/interfaces/store/test_llm_framework.py` and any
   `llm`/findings tests.
2. **F7:** give `make_error_dict` a shared base — e.g. start from
   `make_initial_state()` (or a `_base_ticket_fields()` builder) and override the
   error-specific fields. **Note (review):** `make_error_dict` is NOT a clean subset
   — besides omitting `preconditions_summary` it *adds* an `error` key and a
   templated `title`; the shared-base extraction must preserve those error-specific
   keys (override-after-base, don't drop them). This *fixes the
   `preconditions_summary` omission for free*. Add a test asserting both dicts carry
   the same base key set.
3. **F5:** extract `build_blocked_by(ticket_states) -> dict` into the **existing**
   `src/rebar/graph/_blockers.py` (it already holds `_find_direct_blockers` — do
   NOT create a new file) and call it from both `_ready.py` and `_unblock.py`. Run
   `tests/scripts/test_ticket_unblock.py` + `tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_links.py`.
   **Sequencing:** land S5 (which changes blocker semantics / fail-closed in
   `_find_direct_blockers`) **before** this F5 extraction, so the shared helper is
   factored on top of S5's behavior, not under it.
4. **F2/F3:** make `transition.py` adopt `_seam.require_id` / `_seam.require_not_ghost`
   (the seam may need an `fmt`-aware variant that emits the JSON envelope — add it
   to `_seam` and have transition/claim/compact all use it). Fix `compact.py:186`
   to emit the `ticket_not_found` envelope. Verify parity across CLI/lib/MCP for
   the not-found case (`tests/interfaces/`).
5. **F4:** extract the CAS frame into a single
   `_with_detached_tickets_worktree(repo_root, commit_msg, mutate_fn)` (or
   `_mutate_and_advance(..., mutate_fn)`) where `mutate_fn` performs the
   write-or-rm. The shared helper owns the whole conditional-commit-and-CAS tail
   (capture `old_sha` at frame top; produce `new_sha` inside the mutate; CAS
   `update-ref`). **Review caveat — this is NOT a pure mechanical lift:** the two
   copies differ in their commit predicate *in kind* — the write path uses
   `git diff --cached --quiet` (staged-diff), the delete path uses
   `file_path.exists()`. Normalize delete to the **staged-diff guard** (sound: an
   `rm` of an absent file stages nothing, so the guard no-ops identically). State
   this predicate normalization explicitly — "keep behavior byte-identical" and
   "normalize the predicate" are in mild tension and the implementer must know the
   change is intended. Concurrency-critical: do this LAST, review CAS retry +
   cleanup ordering, and run the advisory-lock suite (`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/**`
   relating to the lock).

## Tradeoffs

- **Benefit:** each fix collapses a fix-twice hazard; F7 fixes a live drift bug;
  F4 removes a concurrency-critical copy where a CAS bug would otherwise need
  two edits.
- **Cost/risk:** F4 touches concurrency-critical code — do it last, with the lock
  tests as guardrail, and keep the extracted frame behavior byte-identical. F2/F3
  requires adding an `fmt`-aware `_seam` helper so the JSON envelope is preserved —
  verify interface parity. Everything else is low-risk same-layer extraction.
- **Do not over-DRY:** leave the parallel typed signatures of the three interfaces
  and the genuinely-inverting differs alone.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `code_review.py` and `spec_scan.py` call `finalize_findings`; `_SEVERITY_RANK`
  is defined once.
- [ ] `make_error_dict` and `make_initial_state` share a base; both carry
  `preconditions_summary` and the same key set (test-asserted).
- [ ] The `blocked_by` inversion exists once and both `_ready.py`/`_unblock.py` use it.
- [ ] `transition.py`/`claim`/`compact.py` use the shared `_seam` id/ghost helpers;
  `compact.py` emits the `ticket_not_found` envelope (parity test passes).
- [ ] The advisory-lock CAS frame is extracted once; the lock test suite passes.
- [ ] ruff + mypy + the affected suites stay green; no behavior change except the
  `compact` not-found envelope and the F7 `preconditions_summary` fix.
</content>
data.title: DRY the validated duplication clusters (F9/F4/F5/F2-F3/F7)
